annotation_basis: Blind annotation from source O&M text only, without viewing any experimental results or model outputs.

concept_ground_truth.label: T1
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: At the left-rear coolant-plate outlet on Aurex BatteryHub-612 LR, record the complaint as seepage, dried residue, or undertray drip, and capture the coolant concentration, pack serial, and the last chiller or hose service that touched this branch. Save the as-found stain path from the outlet neck to the shield edge before any clip, hose, or connector body is moved.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T1 retained verbatim from the source document.

concept_ground_truth.label: T2
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: Make the pack safe, remove the local underbody shield, and expose the aluminum outlet neck, PA12 quick-connector shell, green EPDM O-ring seat, stainless retainer clip, hose saddle bracket, and nearby harness fir-tree clip without changing the hose angle. Keep the connector-to-bracket path intact so the original side load can still be judged.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T2 retained verbatim from the source document.

concept_ground_truth.label: T3
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: Clean only the loose dirt and then trace the residue from the lowest drip point upward with a lamp, separating wetting at the latch window from wetting at the tube bead or the cold-plate seam. Residue that begins above the retainer clip and then runs down the shell suggests a different boundary than residue that forms first at the aluminum neck.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T3 retained verbatim from the source document.

concept_ground_truth.label: T4
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: Cap the neighboring branch and apply a controlled pressure hold to the suspect section, then watch the connector shell, O-ring land, and plate seam for the first fresh bead. Record the applied pressure and the time to first wetting so the leak proof is tied to one repeatable condition.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T4 retained verbatim from the source document.

concept_ground_truth.label: T5
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: Compare insertion depth, retainer-clip seating, hose twist, and bracket position against the right-rear outlet on the same pack because a coupler held 3 mm short of full engagement can mimic a seam leak. Use the opposite-side outlet as the geometry reference before deciding which part is actually failed.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T5 retained verbatim from the source document.

concept_ground_truth.label: T6
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: Correct only the identified interface by renewing the O-ring, replacing the cracked connector shell, deburring the outlet bead, or resetting the saddle bracket so the hose leaves the neck without upward pull. Confirm that the retainer clip locks fully into both grooves and that the bracket no longer pushes the shell off axis.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T6 retained verbatim from the source document.

concept_ground_truth.label: T7
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: Circulate coolant through the restored branch and compare inlet and outlet temperatures plus the local shell condition with the pre-repair state while the branch remains visible. The proof is incomplete if the connector stays dry at static pressure but begins to sweat once flow and vibration return.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T7 retained verbatim from the source document.

concept_ground_truth.label: T8
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: Release this branch only when the note names the exact failed boundary, such as O-ring seat, connector shell, retainer clip engagement, outlet bead, or saddle bracket side load, and includes the pressure and circulation results taken after repair. The accepted condition is a dry left-rear outlet path with stable hose alignment and no renewed staining from neck to shield edge.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T8 retained verbatim from the source document.
